2605.04764.title: Elicitation Matters: How Prompts and Query Protocols Shape LLM Surrogates under Sparse Observations
2605.04764.paper_summary: Large language models are increasingly used as surrogate models for low-data optimization, but their optimizer-facing prediction and its uncertainty remain poorly understood. We study the surrogate belief elicited from an LLM under sparse observations, showing that it depends strongly on prompt text and query protocol. We introduce an uncertainty-alignment criterion that measures whether model uncertainty tracks residual ambiguity among sample-consistent functions. Across controlled inference tasks and Bayesian optimization studies, we find that structural prompts act as effective priors, POINTWISE and JOINT querying induce different beliefs, and sequential evidence leads to non-monotonic, order-sensitive confidence updates. These effects change downstream acquisition decisions and regret, showing that elicitation protocol is part of the LLM surrogate specification, not a formatting detail.
2605.04764.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被用作低數據優化的替代模型，但它們面向優化器的預測及其不確定性仍然不甚明瞭。<lb/>我們研究了在稀疏觀測下從LLM中引發的替代信念，顯示它強烈依賴於提示文本和查詢協議。<lb/>我們引入了一個不確定性對齊標準，該標準衡量模型不確定性是否追蹤樣本一致函數之間的殘餘模糊性。<lb/>在受控推理任務和貝葉斯優化研究中，我們發現結構性提示作為有效的先驗，逐點和聯合查詢會產生不同的信念，而序列證據導致非單調的、有序敏感的信心更新。<lb/>這些效應改變了下游的獲取決策和遺憾，顯示引發協議是LLM替代規範的一部分，而不是格式細節。
2605.04764.author: Ge Lei et.al.
